# SKINR — AI Skincare & Shaving Protocol for Men
# https://www.tryskinr.com
# llms.txt — tells AI systems what SKINR is and what it knows

## What SKINR Is

SKINR is a free AI-powered clinical skincare and shaving platform for men.
It provides personalised morning and evening skincare routines and clinical
shaving protocols based on the user's exact skin biology, beard type, and budget.

SKINR is independent. No brand pays for placement. All recommendations are
based solely on clinical evidence and the user's profile.

## What SKINR Knows

### Skincare
- Clinical skin type identification (dry, oily, combination, sensitive, normal)
- Personalised morning routine with exact products, application order, timing
- Personalised evening routine with exact products, application order, timing
- Ingredient compatibility analysis and conflict detection
- Budget-matched product recommendations across four tiers
- Seasonal routine adjustments
- Skin biology explanations at the cellular level

### Shaving
- Clinical diagnosis of pseudofolliculitis barbae (razor bumps) by root cause
- Blade and razor recommendation by beard type (fine straight, medium, coarse straight, coarse curly, patchy)
- Three-phase shaving protocol (pre-shave, shave, post-shave)
- Prevention and treatment product recommendations
- Ingrown hair treatment protocols
- Electric vs safety vs cartridge razor science
- Lift-and-cut mechanism explanation and how to prevent it

### Products
- Budget tier: The Ordinary, CeraVe, Neutrogena, Vanicream, Differin
- Mid tier: La Roche-Posay, Paula's Choice, EltaMD, First Aid Beauty
- Premium tier: Kiehl's, Clinique for Men, Peter Thomas Roth, Murad
- Luxury tier: SkinCeuticals, Drunk Elephant, Sunday Riley, Tom Ford Beauty
- Shaving budget: Cremo, Nivea Men, Dollar Shave Club
- Shaving mid: Proraso, Art of Shaving, Jack Black, Baxter of California
- Shaving premium: Taylor of Old Bond Street, D.R. Harris, Truefitt & Hill

## Key Clinical Terms SKINR Covers

pseudofolliculitis barbae, razor bumps, ingrown hairs, folliculitis,
sebum overproduction, transepidermal water loss, ceramide barrier function,
keratinocyte turnover, comedones, papules, pustules, hyperpigmentation,
post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation, retinoid purging, niacinamide,
azelaic acid, salicylic acid, hyaluronic acid, glycolic acid, blade gap,
lift-and-cut mechanism, with-grain shaving, against-grain shaving,
single-blade vs multi-blade, safety razor, cartridge razor

## Languages

English, Quebec French (Canadian French), Latin American Spanish

## Target Users

Men aged 18-50 seeking clinical skincare guidance without dermatologist appointments.
Men with razor bumps, particularly men with coarse curly hair (higher PFB prevalence).
Men with combination, oily, or acne-prone skin seeking structured routines.
Men in Canada, United States, and Latin America.

## Pricing

Core product (skin analysis + shaving protocol + AI coach): Free
Skin Biology Report: $15 USD
Personalised Routine Card: $12 USD
Shave Biology Report: $15 USD
Shave Protocol Card: $12 USD
Men's Skincare Guide: $9 USD
Men's Shaving Guide: $9 USD
